highlights: Multi-tiered waterfall with crystal-clear water and scenic mountain views in remote Bình Liêu
Free entrance with bike parking available; accessible by motorbike or car from nearby towns
Trekking destination featuring slippery rocks and surrounding ethnic minority villages

thingsToKnow.crowds: Not crowded; peaceful and underdeveloped compared to popular tourist sites
thingsToKnow.difficulty: Easy access by road but slippery rocks require careful footing; some road construction ongoing
thingsToKnow.safety: Located near China border in Bình Liêu District; rocks are slippery when wet
thingsToKnow.accessibility: 30 minutes by motorbike from Bình Liêu town center; road quality varies with some rough sections under development
thingsToKnow.seasonal: January shows lower water levels; summer offers cool refreshing water for swimming

visitorTips: Wear footwear with good grip; rocks become extremely slippery when wet
Visit during summer months for better swimming conditions and water flow
Combine with Hoành Mô loop or stay in Tiên Yên/Bình Liêu town for multi-day exploration

faq.question: Is there an entrance fee to visit the waterfall?
faq.answer: No, entrance is free. Bike parking costs 10,000 VND. A food village is being developed nearby.

faq.question: How do I reach the waterfall from Ha Long or nearby towns?
faq.answer: By motorbike (30 minutes from Bình Liêu center) or car. Road is drivable but some sections are rough and under construction.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit for swimming?
faq.answer: Summer offers the most water and cooler temperatures. January has lower water levels but still visitable.

sentiment.positive: Overwhelmingly positive reviews praise the waterfall's natural beauty, crystal-clear water, multi-tiered structure, and peaceful undeveloped atmosphere ideal for trekking and escaping crowds
